project_goal: Abu Abed Box — Saudi Arabic multiplayer party game platform with 17 games, Node.js + Express + Socket.IO, vanilla JS/CSS client
session_assumptions: Target audience: Saudi Arabian young adults (18-35) at group gatherings
Stack: Node.js 18+, Express 4, Socket.IO 4, vanilla JS, CSS3, no frameworks
All UI Arabic-first, RTL layout, mobile-controller + TV-display pattern
Design Bible v1.0 defines all 17 game palettes, typography, moods, cultural elements
Existing codebase: ~200KB server.js, full client SPA, 6 working games
Deployment: Render.com / Railway free tier initially
Legal: inspired-by Jackbox function, original expression throughout
recommended_build_order: 2. Implement Quiplash CSS component library (.ql-* classes)
3. Build split-screen voting UI + vote stacking bars
4. Implement staged reveal sequence with gauge animation
5. Add safety quip system (server + client + content)
7. Build Web Audio API synthesizer for UI SFX
8. Implement round interstitials and game flow transitions
9. Build audience live polling for Guesspionage
11. Create SVG pattern library (Al Sadu, geometric, mashrabiya)
12. Commission external visual assets from briefs
13. Commission external audio assets from specs
first_5_actions: Fix bugs 1-4: Guesspionage scoring, player threshold, round count, maxRounds
Fix bugs 5-8: slider stepper, extended timers, audience, gauge animation
Implement .ql-* CSS components (split-screen, speech bubbles, vote bars, QUIPLASH overlay)
Build staged reveal sequence with timed beats and gauge needle
Expand ar-SA.json with 60+ questions, safety quips, commentary lines
